</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><B>Note 4:&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Recent Accounting Pronouncements </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B>ASUs Adopted: </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>New
Revenue Standard </I></B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company adopted the New Revenue Standard on a modified retrospective basis on January&nbsp;1, 2018. The
cumulative-effect adjustment related to the timing of revenue recognition on certain product development agreements recorded to beginning retained earnings and accrued expenses as of January&nbsp;1, 2018, was $2.1&nbsp;million. The Company expects
the ongoing impact of the New Revenue Standard to be immaterial to the consolidated financial statements. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>ASU <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">No.&nbsp;2017-09</FONT> - Scope of Modification Accounting (&#147;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2017-09&#148;)</FONT> </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In May 2017, the FASB&nbsp;issued ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">No.&nbsp;2017-09</FONT> to reduce diversity in practice and provide
clarity regarding existing guidance in ASC 718, &#147;Stock Compensation.&#148; The amendments clarify that an entity should apply modification accounting in response to a change in the terms and conditions of an entity&#146;s share-based payment
awards unless three newly specified criteria are met. The amendments are effective for fiscal years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2017, including interim periods within those fiscal years. The Company adopted ASU
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2017-09</FONT> during the first quarter of 2018. The adoption of this standard did not have a material impact on the consolidated financial statements. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>ASU No <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2017-07</FONT> </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company adopted ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2017-07</FONT> during the first quarter of 2018, applying the standard
retrospectively to all periods presented. The adoption of this standard did not have a material impact on the current period or prior period consolidated financial statements. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">111 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">No.&nbsp;2016-18</FONT> - Statement of Cash Flows
(Topic 230): Restricted Cash (a consensus of the FASB Emerging Issues Task Force) (&#147;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-18&#148;)</FONT> </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In November 2016, the FASB issued ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-18,</FONT> which requires entities to include in their cash and
cash-equivalent balances in the statement of cash flows those amounts that are deemed to be restricted cash and restricted cash equivalents. The ASU does not define the terms &#147;restricted cash&#148; and &#147;restricted cash
equivalents.&#148;&nbsp;The amendments are effective for fiscal years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2017, including interim periods within those fiscal years. The Company adopted ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-18</FONT> during the
first quarter of 2018, applying the standard retrospectively to all periods presented. The adoption of this standard did not have a material impact on the current period or prior period consolidated financial statements. See Note 18:
&#147;Supplemental Disclosures&#148; for further information on the adoption of ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-18.</FONT> </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">No.&nbsp;2016-16</FONT> - Income Taxes (Topic 740): Intra-Entity Transfers of Assets Other Than
Inventory (&#147;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-16&#148;)</FONT> </I></B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In October 2016, the FASB issued ASU <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-16,</FONT> which eliminates the exception for all intra-entity sales of assets other than inventory. As a result, a reporting entity would recognize the tax expense from the sale of the asset in the seller&#146;s tax
jurisdiction when the transfer occurs, even though the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">pre-tax</FONT> effects of that transaction are eliminated in consolidation. Any deferred tax asset that arises in the buyer&#146;s jurisdiction would also be
recognized at the time of the transfer. The new guidance does not apply to intra-entity transfers of inventory. The income tax consequences from the sale of inventory from one member of a consolidated entity to another will continue to be deferred
until the inventory is sold to a third-party.&nbsp;The amendments are effective for fiscal years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2017, including interim periods within those fiscal years. The Company adopted ASU
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-16</FONT> during the first quarter of 2018 and recorded the cumulative-effect adjustment of $1.4&nbsp;million as a reduction to the beginning retained earnings. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>ASU No. <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-15</FONT> - Statement of Cash Flows (Topic 230): Classification of Certain Cash Receipts
and Cash Payments (&#147;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-15&#148;)</FONT> </I></B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In August 2016, the FASB issued ASU <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-15,</FONT> which changes how certain cash receipts and cash payments are presented and classified in the statement of cash flows. The amendments are effective for fiscal years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2017,
including interim periods within those fiscal years. The Company adopted ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-15</FONT> during the first quarter of 2018. The adoption of this standard did not have a material impact on the consolidated financial
statements. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">112 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B>ASUs Pending Adoption: </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">No.&nbsp;2017-12</FONT> - Derivatives and Hedging (Topic 815): Targeted Improvements to Accounting
for Hedging Activities (&#147;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2017-12&#148;)</FONT> </I></B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In August 2017, the FASB&nbsp;issued ASU
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">No.&nbsp;2017-12</FONT> to better align hedge accounting with risk management strategies, and as a result, more hedging strategies will be eligible for hedge accounting. Public business entities will have until the
end of the first quarter in which a hedge is designated to perform an initial assessment of a hedge&#146;s effectiveness. After initial qualification, the new guidance permits a qualitative effectiveness assessment for certain hedges instead of a
quantitative test if the company can reasonably support an expectation of high effectiveness throughout the term of the hedge. An initial quantitative test to establish that the hedge relationship is highly effective is still required. The
amendments are effective for fiscal years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2018. Early&nbsp;adoption is permitted. The Company does not anticipate the adoption of&nbsp;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2017-12</FONT> will have a material impact
on its consolidated financial statements. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>ASU No. <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-02</FONT> - Leases (Topic 842) (&#147;ASU <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-02&#148;),</FONT> ASU No. <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-10</FONT> - Codification improvements to Topic 842, Leases (&#147;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-10&#148;),</FONT> ASU No. <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-11</FONT> - Leases (Topic 842) (&#147;ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-11&#148;)</FONT> (collectively, the &#147;New Leasing Standard&#148;) </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In February 2016, the FASB issued ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-02,</FONT> which amended the accounting treatment for leases. ASU <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-02</FONT> requires that a lessee should recognize on its balance sheet a liability to make lease payments (the lease liability) and a
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">right-of-use</FONT></FONT> asset representing its right to use the underlying asset for the lease term. Lessees must apply a modified retrospective transition approach for leases
existing at, or entered into after, the beginning of the earliest comparative period presented in the financial statements. The modified retrospective approach would not require any transition accounting for leases that expired before the earliest
comparative period presented. Lessees and lessors may not apply a full retrospective transition approach. In July 2018, the FASB issued ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-10</FONT> and ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-11.</FONT> ASU <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-10</FONT> provides certain areas for improvement in ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2016-02</FONT> and ASU <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">2018-11</FONT> provides an additional optional transition method by
allowing entities to initially apply the New Leasing Standard at the adoption date and recognize a cumulative-effect adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings in the period of adoption. The New Leasing Standard is effective for fiscal
years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2018, including interim periods within those fiscal years and early adoption is permitted. The Company will adopt the standard beginning January&nbsp;1, 2019 utilizing the modified retrospective transition
method through a cumulative-effect adjustment at the beginning of the first quarter of 2019. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company currently plans to apply the
package of practical expedients to leases that commenced before the effective date whereby we will elect to not reassess the following: (i)&nbsp;whether any expired or existing contracts contain leases; (ii)&nbsp;the lease classification for any
expired or existing leases; and (iii)&nbsp;initial direct costs for any existing leases. The Company expects the adoption of this standard will result in the inclusion of a significant component of the Company&#146;s future minimum lease
obligations, as disclosed in Note 13: &#147;Commitments and Contingencies&#148; on its Consolidated Balance Sheets, as <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">right-of-use</FONT></FONT> assets and lease liabilities with no
material impact to its Consolidated Statements of Operations and Comprehensive Income. The Company is continuing to assess the potential impacts of the New Leasing Standard. We anticipate disclosing additional information, as necessary, to comply
with the New Leasing Standard. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">113 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><B>Note 5:&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Acquisitions, Divestitures and Licensing Transactions </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company pursues strategic acquisitions and divestitures from time to time to leverage its existing capabilities and further build its
business. Acquisition costs are not included as components of consideration transferred and instead are accounted for as expenses in the period in which the costs are incurred. During the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2018 and 2017, the Company
incurred acquisition and divestiture related costs of approximately $4.5&nbsp;million and $3.2&nbsp;million, respectively, which are included in operating expenses on the Company&#146;s Consolidated Statements of Operations and Comprehensive Income.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>2018 Acquisition </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On May&nbsp;8, 2018, the Company acquired 100% of the outstanding shares of SensL Technologies Ltd. (&#147;SensL&#148;), a company
specializing in silicon photomultipliers, single photon avalanche diode and LiDAR sensing products for the automotive, medical, industrial and consumer markets, for $71.6&nbsp;million, funded with cash on hand. This acquisition positions the Company
to extend its products in automotive sensing applications for ADAS and autonomous driving by adding LiDAR capabilities to the Company&#146;s existing capabilities in imaging and radar. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The following table presents the allocation of the purchase price of SensL for the assets acquired and liabilities assumed based on their fair
values (in millions): </P> <P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

completion of the related projects. The value assigned to IPRD was determined by estimating the net cash flows from the projects when completed and discounting the net cash flows to their present value using a discount rate of 30.0%. The cash flows
from IPRD&#146;s significant products are expected to commence in 2019. </P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The acquisition produced $18.9&nbsp;million of goodwill, which was allocated to the
Intelligent Sensing Group.&nbsp;Goodwill is attributable to a combination of SensL&#146;s assembled workforce, expectations regarding a more meaningful engagement by the customers due to the scale of the combined company and other product and
operating synergies. Goodwill will not be amortized but instead tested for impairment at least annually (more frequently if certain indicators are present). Goodwill arising from the SensL acquisition is not deductible for tax purposes. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Unaudited <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">pro-forma</FONT> consolidated results of operations for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2018 and
2017 are not included considering the significance of the acquisition to the results of the Company. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>2018 Divestiture </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On June&nbsp;25, 2018, the Company divested the transient voltage suppressing diodes business it acquired from Fairchild to TSC America, Inc.
for $5.6&nbsp;million in cash and recorded a gain of $4.6&nbsp;million after writing off the carrying values of the assets and liabilities disposed. There were certain other immaterial transactions resulting in a total gain of $5.0&nbsp;million
during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2018. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>Licensing Transactions </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">During 2016 and 2017, the Company entered into an Asset Purchase Agreement with Huaian Imaging Device Manufacturer Corporation
(&#147;HIDM&#148;) pursuant to which the Company received $52.5&nbsp;million in cash in 2017 and provided perpetual, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-exclusive</FONT> licenses relating to certain technologies to HIDM. Of this amount,
$10.0&nbsp;million was recorded as deferred licensing income to be recognized in the period in which certain qualification requirements of the technologies are achieved (or refunded to HIDM, if such qualification did not occur by June&nbsp;21,
2018). Prior to this date, the Company achieved such qualification requirements for the technologies transferred and recognized $10.0&nbsp;million as licensing income. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On November&nbsp;29, 2017, the Company and QST Co. Ltd (&#147;QST&#148;) entered into an IP license and technology transfer agreement
(&#147;IP Agreement&#148;) to grant QST patent licenses and IP rights to certain of the Company&#146;s technologies. Pursuant to the IP Agreement, QST receives perpetual, worldwide, nonexclusive and nontransferable patents licenses and IP rights
upon the payment of license fees of $13.0&nbsp;million and other fees of $8.5&nbsp;million in its entirety. Such amounts were paid by QST during the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2017 and 2018. As a result, the Company recognized licensing income of
$22.7&nbsp;million relating to licensing and other fees and certain other aspects of the transaction during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2018. The Company also recognized certain immaterial amounts of licensing income relating to other
transactions during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2018. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">115 </P>

</DIV></Center>

<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>2017 Divestiture </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On September&nbsp;29, 2017, the Company entered into a Share Purchase Agreement with mCube, whereby mCube acquired 100% of the outstanding
shares of Xsens Holding B.V., a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company, for cash consideration of $26.0&nbsp;million (collectively, the &#147;Xsens Transaction&#148;). Twenty percent of the consideration, or $5.2&nbsp;million, was deposited into an
escrow account and the remaining $20.8&nbsp;million was received on September&nbsp;29, 2017. There were no indemnification liabilities identified, and the escrow amount has been considered as part of the consideration in calculating the gain and
included in other current assets in the Consolidated Balance Sheet as of December&nbsp;31, 2018. The escrow amount will be released to the Company upon satisfaction of any pending claims eighteen months after the date on which the Xsens Transaction
closed. The Company recorded a gain of $12.5&nbsp;million after writing off the carrying value of the assets and liabilities sold of $7.0&nbsp;million and goodwill of $6.5&nbsp;million. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>2016 Acquisition </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On September&nbsp;19, 2016, the Company acquired 100% of Fairchild, whereby Fairchild became a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company. The
purchase price totaled $2,532.2&nbsp;million in cash and was funded by the Company&#146;s borrowings against its Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility and a partial draw of the Revolving Credit Facility, as well as with cash on hand. See Note 9:
&#147;Long-Term Debt&#148; for additional information. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">For the period from September&nbsp;19, 2016 to December&nbsp;31, 2016, the Company
recognized revenue of $411.5&nbsp;million and a net loss of $34.5&nbsp;million relating to Fairchild, which included charges for the amortization of fair market value <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">step-up</FONT> of inventory of
$67.5&nbsp;million, the amortization of acquired intangible assets and restructuring. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">116 </P>

<TD VALIGN="bottom">&nbsp;&nbsp;</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"> <P STYLE=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; border-top:3.00px double #000000">&nbsp;</P></TD>

<TD>&nbsp;</TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Acquired intangible assets included $134.2&nbsp;million of IPRD assets, which are to be amortized over the
useful life upon successful completion of the related projects. The value assigned to IPRD was determined by considering the importance of products under development to the overall development plan, estimating costs to develop the purchased IPRD
into commercially viable products, estimating the resulting net cash flows from the projects when completed and discounting the net cash flows to their present value. The Company utilized a discount rate of 14.5% and cash flows from its significant
products were expected to commence from 2017 and beyond. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Other acquired intangible assets of $413.6&nbsp;million consisted of developed
technology of $272.7&nbsp;million (eleven year weighted-average useful life), customer relationships of $135.5&nbsp;million (fifteen year useful life) and backlog of $3.0&nbsp;million (six month useful life). The total weighted-average amortization
period for the acquired intangibles is 12.1 years. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The acquisition produced $656.1&nbsp;million of goodwill, of which $366.1&nbsp;million
was assigned to the Power Solutions Group and $290.0&nbsp;million to the Analog Solutions Group. Goodwill is attributable to a combination of Fairchild&#146;s assembled workforce, expectations regarding a more meaningful engagement with customers
due to the scale of the combined Company and other synergies. Goodwill arising from the Fairchild acquisition is not deductible for tax purposes. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2016, the Company incurred $24.7&nbsp;million in acquisition-related costs from the Fairchild
acquisition. These costs are recorded in general and administrative expense in the Consolidated Statements of Operations and Comprehensive Income. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">117 </P>

</DIV></Center>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>2016 Divestiture </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On August&nbsp;25, 2016, the U.S. Federal Trade Commission (&#147;FTC&#148;) accepted a proposed consent order whereby, prior to the closing
of the acquisition of Fairchild, the FTC required the Company to dispose of its IGBT business.&nbsp;In satisfaction of this requirement, on August&nbsp;29, 2016, the Company sold the ignition IGBT business to Littelfuse. On the same day, the Company
sold its transient voltage suppression diode and switching thyristor product lines (&#147;Thyristor&#148;) to Littelfuse.&nbsp;The sale of the ignition IGBT and Thyristor businesses was for $104.0&nbsp;million in cash.&nbsp;In&nbsp;connection with
the sale, the Company recorded a gain on divestiture of $92.2&nbsp;million after, among other things, transferring inventory of $4.1&nbsp;million to Littelfuse, writing off goodwill of $3.4&nbsp;million and deferring $4.3&nbsp;million of the
proceeds representing the fair value of manufacturing services which has been recognized in the Consolidated Statements of Operations and Comprehensive Income through December&nbsp;31, 2018. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><B>Note 6:&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Goodwill and Intangible Assets </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>Goodwill </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Goodwill is tested for impairment at the reporting unit level, which is one level below the Company&#146;s operating segments. The Company
performed qualitative assessments for the annual impairment analysis during the fourth quarters of 2018 and 2017 and concluded that it is more likely than not that the fair value of its reporting units exceed their carrying amounts and a
quantitative impairment test was not required. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">118 </P>

</DIV></Center>

projects under the Intelligent Sensing Group was impaired and recorded a charge of $3.5&nbsp;million, and the Company also completed certain of its IPRD projects resulting in the reclassification of $10.4&nbsp;million from IPRD to developed

the Power Solutions Group and Analog Solutions Group and recorded impairment losses of $7.7&nbsp;million. Additionally, the Company determined that the value of certain of its projects under the Analog Solutions Group were impaired and recorded
charges of $5.4&nbsp;million. During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2017, the Company also completed certain of its IPRD projects, resulting in the reclassification of $99.4&nbsp;million from IPRD to developed technology and, disposed of
$8.7&nbsp;million of intangible assets as part of the Xsens Transaction. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2016, the Company
canceled certain of its previously capitalized IPRD projects under the Intelligent Sensing Group and recorded impairment losses of $2.2&nbsp;million, and the Company also completed certain of its IPRD projects resulting in the reclassification of
$21.6&nbsp;million from IPRD to developed technology. </P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company did not have any significant restructuring activities during the year ended December&nbsp;31,
2018. Activity related to the Company&#146;s significant restructuring programs that were initiated during 2016 or 2017 was as follows: </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Post-Fairchild Acquisition Restructuring Costs </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Following the acquisition of Fairchild, the Company approved the implementation of a cost-reduction plan, which eliminated approximately 225
positions from its workforce as a result of redundancies. Restructuring charges of $25.7&nbsp;million were recorded during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2016. During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2017, an additional 111 positions were
eliminated, totaling 336 pursuant to the plan. As of December&nbsp;31, 2017, a total of 331 employees had exited, and the remaining five exited during 2018. The restructuring expense attributable to severance and termination benefits was
$7.9&nbsp;million and to other exit costs was $1.8&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2017. The total expense for this program amounted to $35.4&nbsp;million and the Company paid $13.4&nbsp;million and $20.2&nbsp;million during the
years ended December&nbsp;31, 2017 and 2016, respectively. Accrued severance benefits for this program was $1.8&nbsp;million as of December&nbsp;31, 2017, of which $1.3&nbsp;million was paid during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2018. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Manufacturing Relocation </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">During March 2016, the Company announced a plan to relocate certain of its manufacturing operations to another existing location. During the
quarter ended March&nbsp;31, 2017, the Company made the decision to cancel the plans for relocation and announced all workforce would remain intact. As a result, the accrued balance of $2.1&nbsp;million was released as of March&nbsp;31, 2017. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Former System Solutions Group Segment Voluntary Workforce Reduction </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">During the quarter ended June&nbsp;30, 2017, the Company announced a voluntary resignation program for the former System Solutions Group. A
total of 36 employees had signed employee separation agreements as of December&nbsp;31, 2017 and the related expense for the year was $2.2&nbsp;million, of which $2.0&nbsp;million had been paid as of December&nbsp;31, 2017. The remaining amounts
were paid during 2018. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">123 </P>

</DIV></Center>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Assets classified as
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">held-for-sale,</FONT></FONT> consisting primarily of properties, are required to be recorded at the lower of carrying value or fair value less any costs to sell. The carrying value of
these assets as of December&nbsp;31, 2018 and 2017 was $1.4&nbsp;million and $5.3&nbsp;million, respectively, and is reported as other current assets on the Company&#146;s Consolidated Balance Sheet.&nbsp;The Company sold the assets <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">held-for-sale</FONT></FONT> at December&nbsp;31, 2017 in January 2018 for $5.5&nbsp;million. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Depreciation expense for property, plant and equipment, including amortization of capital leases, totaled $359.3&nbsp;million,
$325.2&nbsp;million and $239.6&nbsp;million for 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">As of December&nbsp;31, 2018 and 2017, total property,
plant and equipment included $0.9&nbsp;million and $4.2&nbsp;million, respectively, of assets financed under capital leases. Accumulated depreciation associated with these assets is included in total accumulated depreciation in the table above. </P>

<TD VALIGN="bottom"></TD>

<TD>&nbsp;</TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>Amended Credit Agreement </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Fairchild Transaction Financing </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On April&nbsp;15, 2016, the Company obtained capital for the Fairchild Transaction purchase consideration and other general corporate purposes
by entering into the Amended Credit Agreement and the Guarantee and Collateral Agreement. The proceeds from the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility, along with $67.7&nbsp;million funded by the Company, were deposited into escrow accounts until the
close of the Fairchild Transaction. Upon the close of the Fairchild Transaction, the Company&#146;s then current senior revolving credit facility was terminated and replaced by the Revolving Credit Facility, which became immediately available to the
Company. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The acquisition of Fairchild was funded with proceeds from the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility, Company-funded amounts
previously deposited into escrow accounts, proceeds from a $200.0&nbsp;million draw against the Company&#146;s Revolving Credit Facility and existing cash on hand. Proceeds from the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility were also used to pay for debt
issuance costs, transaction fees and expenses. Borrowings under the Amended Credit Agreement may be incurred in U.S. Dollars, Euros, Pounds Sterling, Japanese Yen or any other currency approved by the Agent and the lenders under the Revolving Credit
Facility, subject to certain qualifications described in the Amended Credit Agreement. Regardless of currency, all borrowings under the Amended Credit Agreement may, at the Company&#146;s option, be incurred as either eurocurrency loans
(&#147;Eurocurrency Loans&#148;) or alternate base rate loans (&#147;ABR Loans&#148;). </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">126 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Amendments to the Amended Credit Agreement </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On September&nbsp;30, 2016, the Company, and certain of the Company&#146;s subsidiaries, as guarantors (the &#147;Guarantors&#148;), entered
into the First Amendment to the Amended Credit Agreement with the several lenders party thereto and Deutsche Bank AG New York Branch, as the administrative agent (the &#147;Agent&#148;). The First Amendment reduced the applicable margins on
Eurocurrency Loans to 2.75% and 3.25% for borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility and the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility, respectively, and reduced applicable margins on ABR Loans to 1.75% and 2.25% for borrowings under the Revolving Credit
Facility and the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility, respectively. Additionally, under the First Amendment: (i)&nbsp;the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility was increased to $2.4&nbsp;billion; (ii)&nbsp;certain restructuring transactions and intercompany
intellectual property transfers were permitted in order to achieve efficient integration of the Company, its subsidiaries and acquired entities; and (iii)&nbsp;certain changes were made to the provisions regarding hedge agreements to allow the
Company and each of the Guarantors to enter into certain hedge arrangements that shall be deemed to be &#147;obligations&#148; for purposes of the Amended Credit Agreement which may be collateralized by the collateral granted pursuant to the
Guarantee and Collateral Agreement. The Company used the additional $200.0&nbsp;million proceeds under the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility to pay off the outstanding balance under the Revolving Credit Facility. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On March&nbsp;31, 2017, the Company, the Guarantors, the several lenders party thereto and the Agent entered into the Second Amendment to the
Amended Credit Agreement (the &#147;Second Amendment&#148;). The Second Amendment provided for, among other things, modifications to the Amended Credit Agreement to allow the 1.625% Notes to rank pari passu with borrowings under the Amended Credit
Agreement and to reduce the interest rates payable under the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility and the Revolving Credit Facility. The Second Amendment reduced the applicable margins on Eurocurrency Loans to 1.75% and 2.25% for borrowings under the
Revolving Credit Facility and the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility, respectively, and reduced the applicable margins on ABR Loans to 0.75% and 1.25% for borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility and the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility,
respectively. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On November&nbsp;30, 2017, the Company, the Guarantors, the several lenders party thereto and the Agent entered into the
Third Amendment to the Amended Credit Agreement (the &#147;Third Amendment&#148;). The Third Amendment provided for, among other things, modifications to the Amended Credit Agreement to reduce the interest rate payable under the Term Loan
&#147;B&#148; Facility and to increase the amount that may be borrowed pursuant to the Revolving Credit Facility to $1.0&nbsp;billion. The Third Amendment reduced the applicable margins on Eurocurrency Loans to&nbsp;1.50% and&nbsp;2.00% for
borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility and the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility, respectively, and reduced applicable margins on ABR Loans to&nbsp;0.50% and&nbsp;1.00% for borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility and the Term Loan
&#147;B&#148; Facility, respectively. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">127 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On May&nbsp;31, 2018, the Company, the Guarantors, the several lenders party thereto and the
Agent entered into the Fourth Amendment to the Amended Credit Agreement (the &#147;Fourth Amendment&#148;). Pursuant to the Fourth Amendment, for any interest period ending after the date of the Fourth Amendment, Eurocurrency Loans will accrue
interest at (i)&nbsp;a base rate per annum equal to the Adjusted LIBO Rate (as defined in the Amended Credit Agreement) plus (ii)&nbsp;an applicable margin equal to (x) 1.25% with respect to borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility (with
step-downs and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">step-ups</FONT> as set forth in the Amended Credit Agreement) or (y) 1.75% with respect to borrowings under the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility. Pursuant to the Fourth Amendment, ABR Loans will accrue
interest at (i)&nbsp;a base rate per annum equal to the highest of (x)&nbsp;the Federal funds rate plus 0.50%, (y) the prime commercial lending rate announced by the Agent from time to time as its prime lending rate and (z)&nbsp;the Adjusted LIBO
Rate for a one month interest period (or if such day is not a business day, the immediately preceding business day) (determined after giving effect to any applicable &#147;floor&#148;) plus 1.00%; provided that, the Adjusted LIBO Rate for any day
shall be based on the LIBO Rate, subject to the interest rate floors set forth in the Amended Credit Agreement, plus (ii)&nbsp;an applicable margin equal to (x) 0.25% with respect to borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility (with step-downs
and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">step-ups</FONT> as set forth in the Amended Credit Agreement) or (y) 0.75% with respect to borrowings under the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The obligations under the Amended Credit Agreement are guaranteed by the Guarantors and collateralized by a pledge of substantially all of the
assets of the Company and the Guarantors, including a pledge of the equity interests in certain of the Company&#146;s domestic and first tier foreign subsidiaries, subject to customary exceptions. The obligations under the Amended Credit Agreement
are also collateralized by mortgage on certain real property assets of the Company and its domestic subsidiaries. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Amended Credit
Agreement includes financial maintenance covenants, including, among others, a maximum total net leverage ratio and a minimum interest coverage ratio. It also contains other customary affirmative and negative covenants and events of default. The
Company was in compliance with its covenants as of December&nbsp;31, 2018. The Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility will mature on March&nbsp;31, 2023 and the Revolving Credit Facility will mature on September&nbsp;19, 2021. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Debt Refinancing and Prepayments </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company incurred third-party, legal and other fees of $1.1&nbsp;million related to the Fourth Amendment and recorded debt extinguishment
charges of $2.6&nbsp;million, which included a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">write-off</FONT> of $1.5&nbsp;million of unamortized debt discount and issuance costs and $1.1&nbsp;million in third-party fees. The Company also prepaid
$70.0&nbsp;million of borrowings under the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2018 and expensed $2.0&nbsp;million of unamortized debt discount and issuance costs attributed to the partial <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">pay-down</FONT> as loss on debt refinancing and prepayment. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company incurred third-party, legal and other
fees of $3.3&nbsp;million related to the Third Amendment and capitalized $1.9&nbsp;million of closing costs relating to the Revolving Credit Facility which will be amortized straight-line over its term and expensed $1.4&nbsp;million of third-party
fees and expenses relating to the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility. The Company also expensed $12.9&nbsp;million of unamortized debt discount and issuance costs attributed to the partial pay down of $400.0&nbsp;million of the Term Loan &#147;B&#148;
Facility. The Company prepaid $200.0&nbsp;million of borrowings under the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2017 and expensed $6.7&nbsp;million of unamortized debt discount and issuance costs attributed to the
partial <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">pay-down</FONT> as loss on debt refinancing and prepayment. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">128 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company incurred legal and other fees of $2.4&nbsp;million related to the Second
Amendment and recorded debt extinguishment charges of $5.6&nbsp;million, which included a $3.2&nbsp;million <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">write-off</FONT> of unamortized debt issuance costs and $2.4&nbsp;million in third-party fees. On
March&nbsp;31, 2017, the Company used the proceeds from the issuance of the 1.625% Notes, amounting to $562.1&nbsp;million, and cash on hand of $12.9&nbsp;million to prepay $575.0&nbsp;million of the outstanding balance of the Term Loan
&#147;B&#148; Facility and expensed $20.6&nbsp;million of unamortized debt discount and issuance costs. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company incurred debt
issuance costs consisting of legal, underwriting and other fees of $66.6&nbsp;million related to the Term Loan &#147;B&#148; Facility, including $22.0&nbsp;million toward lender fees for the First Amendment and recorded debt extinguishment charges
of $4.7&nbsp;million during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2016. The Company incurred debt issuance costs consisting of legal, underwriting and other fees of $8.2&nbsp;million for the&nbsp;Revolving Credit Facility and accounted for the
termination and replacement of its senior revolving credit facility by the Revolving Credit Facility as a debt modification and wrote off $1.6&nbsp;million in unamortized debt issuance costs. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">As a result of the above, the Company recorded debt refinancing and prepayment charges of $4.6&nbsp;million, $47.2&nbsp;million and
$6.3&nbsp;million for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2018, 2017 and 2016, respectively. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>1.00% Notes due 2020 </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On June&nbsp;8, 2015, the Company completed a private placement of $690.0&nbsp;million of its 1.00% Notes to qualified institutional buyers
pursuant to Rule 144A under the Securities Act. The Company was the sole issuer in the private unregistered offering of the 1.00% Notes. The Company incurred issuance costs of $18.3&nbsp;million in connection with the issuance of the 1.00% Notes, of
which $15.4&nbsp;million were recorded as debt issuance costs and are being amortized using the effective interest method and $2.9&nbsp;million were allocated to the conversion option (as further described below) and were recorded to equity. The
1.00% Notes are governed by an indenture between the Company, as the issuer, the guarantors named therein and Wells Fargo Bank, National Association, as trustee (the &#147;1.00% Indenture&#148;). </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company&#146;s use of the net proceeds from the offering included the following: (i)&nbsp;the funding of the cost of the convertible note
hedge transactions described below (the cost of which was partially offset by the proceeds that the Company received from entering into the warrant transactions described below); (ii) funding the repurchase of $70.0&nbsp;million of the
Company&#146;s common stock which was acquired from purchasers of the 1.00% Notes in privately negotiated transactions effected through one or more of the initial purchasers or their affiliates conducted concurrently with the issuance of the 1.00%
Notes; and (iii)&nbsp;repayment of $350.0&nbsp;million of borrowings outstanding under its Revolving Credit Facility. The remainder of the proceeds was intended for general corporate purposes, including additional share repurchases and potential
acquisitions. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The notes bear interest at the rate of 1.00% per year from the date of issuance, payable semiannually in arrears on
June&nbsp;1 and December&nbsp;1 of each year, beginning on December&nbsp;1, 2015. The 1.00% Notes are fully and unconditionally guaranteed on a senior unsecured obligation basis by certain existing subsidiaries of the Company. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">129 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The 1.00% Notes are convertible by holders into cash and shares of the Company&#146;s common
stock at a conversion rate of 54.0643 shares of common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes (subject to adjustment in certain events), which is equivalent to an initial conversion price of $18.50 per share of common stock. The Company will
settle conversion of all 1.00% Notes validly tendered for conversion in cash and shares of the Company&#146;s common stock, if applicable, subject to the Company&#146;s right to pay the share amount in additional cash. Holders may convert their
1.00% Notes only under the following circumstances: (i)&nbsp;during any calendar quarter commencing after the calendar quarter ending on September&nbsp;30, 2015, if the last reported sale price of common stock for at least 20 trading days (whether
or not consecutive) during the period of 30 consecutive trading days ending on the last trading day of the immediately preceding calendar quarter is greater than or equal to 130% of the conversion price on each applicable trading day;
(ii)&nbsp;during the five <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">business-day</FONT> period immediately following any five consecutive <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">trading-day</FONT> period in which the trading price per $1,000 principal amount of
1.00% Notes for each day of such period was less than 98% of the product of the closing sale price of the Company&#146;s common stock and the conversion rate; (iii)&nbsp;upon occurrence of the specified transactions described in the 1.00% Indenture;
or (iv)&nbsp;on and after September&nbsp;1, 2020. Upon conversion of the 1.00% Notes, the Company will deliver cash, shares of its common stock or a combination of cash and shares of its common stock, at the Company&#146;s election. For a discussion
of the dilutive effects for earnings per share calculations, see Note 10: &#147;Earnings Per Share and Equity.&#148; </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The 1.00% Notes will
mature on December&nbsp;1, 2020. If a holder elects to convert its 1.00% Notes in connection with the occurrence of specified fundamental changes that occur prior to September&nbsp;1, 2020, the holder will be entitled to receive, in addition to cash
and shares of common stock equal to the conversion rate, an additional number of shares of common stock, in each case as described in the 1.00% Indenture. Notwithstanding these conversion rate adjustments, the 1.00% Notes contain an explicit limit
on the number of shares issuable upon conversion. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In connection with the occurrence of specified fundamental changes, holders may require
the Company to repurchase for cash all or part of their 1.00% Notes at a purchase price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the 1.00% Notes to be repurchased, plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but not including, the fundamental change
repurchase date. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The 1.00% Notes, which are the Company&#146;s unsecured obligations, ranks equally in right of payment to all of the
Company&#146;s existing and future unsubordinated indebtedness and are senior in right of payment to all of the Company&#146;s existing and future subordinated obligations. The 1.00% Notes are effectively subordinated to any of the Company&#146;s or
its subsidiaries&#146; secured indebtedness to the extent of the value of the assets securing such indebtedness. ON Semiconductor was the sole issuer of the 1.00% Notes. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In accordance with accounting guidance on embedded conversion features, the Company valued and bifurcated the conversion option associated
with the 1.00% Notes from the respective host debt instrument, which is referred to as the debt discount, and initially recorded the conversion option of $110.4&nbsp;million in stockholders&#146; equity. The resulting debt discount is being
amortized to interest expense at an effective interest rate of 4.29% over the contractual terms of the 1.00% Notes. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">130 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company used $56.9&nbsp;million of the net proceeds from the offering of its 1.00% Notes
to concurrently enter into convertible note hedge and warrant transactions with certain of the initial purchasers of the 1.00% Notes. Pursuant to these transactions, the Company has the option to purchase initially (subject to adjustment for certain
specified transactions) a total of 37.3&nbsp;million shares of its common stock at a price of $18.50 per share. The total cost of the convertible note hedge transactions was $108.9&nbsp;million. In addition, the Company sold warrants to certain bank
counterparties whereby the holders of the warrants have the option to purchase initially (subject to adjustment for certain specified events) a total of 37.3&nbsp;million shares of the Company&#146;s common stock at a price of $25.96 per share. The
Company received $52.0&nbsp;million in cash proceeds from the sale of these warrants. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In aggregate, the purchase of the convertible note
hedges and the sale of the warrants are intended to reduce the potential dilution from the conversion of the 1.00% Notes. As these transactions meet certain accounting criteria, the convertible note hedges and warrants are recorded in
stockholders&#146; equity and are not accounted for as derivatives. The net cost incurred in connection with the convertible note hedge and warrant transactions was recorded as a reduction to additional
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">paid-in</FONT> capital in the Consolidated Balance Sheet. All of the shares subject to the conversion of the 1.00% Notes and hedging transactions were reserved in the form of the Company&#146;s treasury stock. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>1.625% Notes due 2023 </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On March&nbsp;31, 2017, the Company completed a private placement of $575.0&nbsp;million of its 1.625% Notes to qualified institutional buyers
pursuant to Rule 144A under the Securities Act. The Company incurred issuance costs of $13.7&nbsp;million in connection with the issuance of the 1.625% Notes, of which $11.1&nbsp;million was capitalized as debt issuance costs and is being amortized
using the effective interest method, and $2.6&nbsp;million was allocated to the conversion option (as further described below) and was recorded as equity. The 1.625% Notes are governed by the 1.625% Indenture. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The net proceeds from the offering of the 1.625% Notes were used to repay $562.1&nbsp;million of borrowings outstanding under the Term Loan
&#147;B&#148; Facility. The 1.625% Notes bear interest at the rate of 1.625%&nbsp;per year from the date of issuance, payable semiannually in arrears on April&nbsp;15 and October&nbsp;15 of each year, beginning on October&nbsp;15, 2017. The 1.625%
Notes are fully and unconditionally guaranteed, on a joint and several basis, by each of the Company&#146;s subsidiaries that is a borrower or guarantor under the Amended Credit Agreement. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">131 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The initial conversion rate of the 1.625% Notes is 48.2567 shares of common stock per $1,000
principal amount of 1.625% Notes (subject to adjustment in certain events), which is equivalent to an initial conversion price of approximately $20.72 per share of common stock. Prior to the close of business on the business day immediately
preceding July&nbsp;15, 2023, the 1.625% Notes will be convertible only under the following circumstances: (1)&nbsp;during any calendar quarter commencing after the calendar quarter ending on June&nbsp;30, 2017 (and only during such calendar
quarter), if the last reported sale price of the Company&#146;s common stock for at least 20 trading days (whether or not consecutive) during the period of 30 consecutive trading days ending on the last trading day of the immediately preceding
calendar quarter is greater than or equal to 130% of the conversion price on each applicable trading day; (2)&nbsp;during the five business day period after any five consecutive trading day period in which the trading price per $1,000 principal
amount of the 1.625% Notes for each trading day of such period was less than 98% of the product of the last reported sale price of the Company&#146;s common stock and the conversion rate on each such trading day; or (3)&nbsp;upon the occurrence of
specified corporate transactions described in the 1.625% Indenture. On or after July&nbsp;15, 2023, until the close of business on the second scheduled trading day immediately preceding the maturity date, holders of the 1.625% Notes may convert all
or a portion of their 1.625% Notes at any time. Upon conversion of the 1.625% Notes, the Company will deliver cash, shares of its common stock or a combination of cash and shares of its common stock, at the Company&#146;s election. For a discussion
of the dilutive effects for earnings per share calculations, see Note 10: &#147;Earnings Per Share and Equity&#148;. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The 1.625% Notes
will mature on October&nbsp;15, 2023. If a holder elects to convert its 1.625% Notes in connection with the occurrence of specified fundamental changes that occur prior to July&nbsp;15, 2023, the holder will be entitled to receive, in addition to
cash and/or shares of common stock equal to the conversion rate, an additional number of shares of common stock, as described in the 1.625% Indenture. Notwithstanding these conversion rate adjustments, the 1.625% Notes contain an explicit limit on
the number of shares issuable upon conversion. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In connection with the occurrence of specified fundamental changes, holders may require
the Company to repurchase for cash all or a portion of their 1.625% Notes at a purchase price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the 1.625% Notes to be repurchased, plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but not including, the fundamental change
repurchase date. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The 1.625% Notes, which are the Company&#146;s unsecured obligations, rank equally in right of payment to all of the
Company&#146;s existing and future unsubordinated indebtedness and are senior in right of payment to all of the Company&#146;s existing and future subordinated obligations. The 1.625% Notes are effectively subordinated to any of the Company&#146;s
or its subsidiaries&#146; secured indebtedness to the extent of the value of the assets securing such indebtedness. ON Semiconductor was the sole issuer of the 1.625% Notes. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">In accordance with accounting guidance on embedded conversion features, the Company valued and bifurcated the conversion option associated
with the 1.625% Notes from the respective host debt instrument, which is referred to as the debt discount, and initially recorded the conversion option of&nbsp;$115.7&nbsp;million in stockholders&#146; equity. The resulting debt discount is being
amortized to interest expense at an effective interest rate of&nbsp;5.38%&nbsp;over the contractual terms of the notes. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">132 </P>

</DIV></Center>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Concurrently with the offering of the 1.625% Notes, the Company used $59.5 million&nbsp;of
borrowings under the Revolving Credit Facility&nbsp;to enter into convertible note hedge and warrant transactions with certain of the initial purchasers of the 1.625% Notes. Pursuant to these transactions, the Company has the option to purchase
(subject to adjustment for certain specified transactions) an aggregate of&nbsp;27.7&nbsp;million shares of its common stock at a price of $20.72&nbsp;per share. The total cost of the convertible note hedge transactions was $144.7&nbsp;million. In
addition, the Company sold warrants to certain bank counterparties whereby the holders of the warrants have the option to purchase initially (subject to adjustment for certain specified events) a total of&nbsp;27.7&nbsp;million shares of the
Company&#146;s common stock at a price of $30.70 per share. The Company received $85.2&nbsp;million in cash proceeds from the sale of these warrants. The tax impact of the conversion option and the convertible note hedge and warrant transactions
amounted to $11.0&nbsp;million and was recorded in stockholders&#146; equity. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">Together, the purchase of the convertible note hedges and
the sale of the warrants are intended to reduce the potential dilution from the conversion of the 1.625% Notes. As these transactions meet certain accounting criteria, the convertible note hedges and warrants are recorded in stockholders&#146;
equity and are not accounted for as derivatives. The net cost incurred in connection with the convertible note hedge and warrant transactions was recorded as a reduction to additional <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">paid-in</FONT> capital in the
consolidated balance sheet. All of the shares subject to the conversion of the 1.625% Notes and hedging transactions were reserved from the Company&#146;s unallocated shares. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>Note Payable to SMBC </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On January&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company amended and restated its seven-year, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-collateralized</FONT> loan
obligation with SANYO Electric. In connection with the amendment and restatement of the loan agreement, SANYO Electric assigned all of its rights under the loan agreement to SMBC. The loan had an original principal amount of approximately
$377.5&nbsp;million and had a principal balance of $122.7&nbsp;million as of December&nbsp;31, 2017. The entire balance was repaid on the due date of January&nbsp;2, 2018. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><B><I>Other Long-term Debt </I></B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Note Payable to Fujitsu </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On October&nbsp;1, 2018, the Company assumed a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">yen-denominated</FONT>
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-collateralized</FONT> loan obligation amounting to $50.6&nbsp;million as a result of the Company acquiring a majority ownership in OSA. See Note 10: &#147;Earnings Per Share and Equity&#148; for more information
on the acquisition of OSA. Amortization and maturity of the loan is at the request of the lender, FSL. The loan bears a variable interest rate which is payable monthly and the ending balance amounting to $51.6&nbsp;million has been classified as
current portion of long-term debt in the Consolidated Balance Sheet as of December&nbsp;31, 2018. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>U.S. Real Estate Mortgages </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On August&nbsp;4, 2014, one of the Company&#146;s U.S. subsidiaries entered into an amended and restated loan agreement with a bank for
approximately $49.4&nbsp;million, which was collateralized by real estate, including certain of the Company&#146;s facilities in California, Oregon, and Idaho. The balance as of December 31, 2018 was $29.5 million and the loan bears interest which
is payable monthly at a rate of approximately 3.12% per annum, with a balloon payment of approximately $26.7&nbsp;million in 2019. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">133 </P>

</DIV></Center>

<Center><DIV STYLE="width:8.5in" align="left">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>ON SEMICONDUCTOR CORPORATION AND SUBSIDIARIES </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center"><B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS&nbsp;-&nbsp;Continued </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:14pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Philippine Term Loans </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">During the second quarter of 2015, the Company&#146;s wholly-owned Philippine subsidiaries and ON Semiconductor, as guarantor, entered into
two <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-collateralized</FONT> term loans with an aggregate borrowing capacity of $50.0&nbsp;million, the terms of which were set forth in agreements by and between the Company&#146;s Philippine subsidiaries and a
Philippine bank. During the third quarter of 2015, the Company borrowed the full $50.0&nbsp;million available under the term loans and the balance was repaid in full during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2018. Borrowings under the loans bear
interest based on the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">3-month</FONT> LIBO Rate plus 2.0% per annum, with interest payable quarterly in arrears. The total borrowed amount must be repaid within five years over 17 equal quarterly principal
installments starting at the end of the fourth quarter from the initial drawdown date. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Malaysia Revolving Line of Credit </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On September&nbsp;23, 2014, one of the Company&#146;s wholly-owned Malaysian subsidiaries and ON Semiconductor, as guarantor, entered into a <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-collateralized</FONT> and uncommitted $25.0&nbsp;million line of credit (the &#147;Malaysia Line of Credit&#148;), the terms of which were set forth in an agreement by and between the Company&#146;s Malaysian
subsidiary and a Japanese bank. During the third quarter of 2014, the Company&#146;s Malaysian subsidiary borrowed the full $25.0&nbsp;million available under the Malaysia Line of Credit. The balance as of December&nbsp;31, 2018 was
$25.0&nbsp;million. Borrowings under the Malaysia Line of Credit bear interest based on the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">3-month</FONT> LIBO Rate, as established at the commencement of each borrowing period, plus 1.45% per annum, with interest
payable quarterly. The borrowed amount is payable within 21 business days of demand. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Vietnam Revolving Line of Credit </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">On September&nbsp;3, 2014, one of the Company&#146;s wholly-owned Vietnamese subsidiaries and ON Semiconductor, as guarantor, entered into a <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-collateralized</FONT> and uncommitted $25.0&nbsp;million line of credit (the &#147;Vietnam Line of Credit&#148;), the terms of which were set forth in an agreement by and between the Company&#146;s Vietnamese
subsidiary and a Japanese bank. As of December&nbsp;31, 2018, the Company&#146;s Vietnamese subsidiary had an outstanding balance of $10.7&nbsp;million under the Vietnam Line of Credit. Borrowings under the Vietnam Line of Credit bear interest based
on the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">3-month</FONT> LIBO Rate and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">12-month</FONT> LIBO Rate, as established at the commencement of each borrowing period, plus 1.45% per annum, with interest payable quarterly and
annually. The outstanding amount is payable within 5 business days of demand. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify"><I>Capital Lease Obligations </I></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:14pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="justify">The Company has various capital lease obligations primarily for buildings, which, as of December&nbsp;31, 2018, totaled $0.9&nbsp;million,
with interest rates ranging from 1.0% to 5.2% and maturities from the first quarter of 2019 until the fourth quarter of 2022. Future payments for the Company&#146;s capital lease obligations are included in the annual maturities table. </P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman" ALIGN="center">134 </P>

</DIV></Center>
